pr.title: Create script to generate wiki translation unit list
pr.author: ribbanya

summary: Added a new Python tool, tools/wiki_tu.py, to generate a Markdown wiki table of translation units from an objdiff-cli JSON report. The script prints file links, matched and total code sizes, fuzzy match percentages, completion status, and placeholder assignee columns for Discord and GitHub. The PR body notes that this can be automated or expanded later.

key_files.change: New executable Python script that reads objdiff-cli JSON and writes a Markdown translation-unit table for the project wiki.

what_changed: Created tools/wiki_tu.py with an argparse-based CLI.
Added a write subcommand that accepts an optional REPORT path argument for a JSON report generated by objdiff-cli.
Supported reading JSON from stdin when report_path is '-'.
Generated a '# Translation Units' Markdown table with columns for file, matched code, total code, percent, completion marker, Discord assignee, and GitHub assignee.
Formatted numeric code sizes with humanfriendly.format_number and fuzzy match percentages with humanfriendly.round_number.
Converted unit names by stripping the leading 'main/' path component and linking rows to ../../tree/master/src/{file}.c.
Rendered complete units with :heavy_check_mark: and incomplete units with :x:.
Left assignee cells as HTML comments so wiki editors can manually fill in Discord and GitHub usernames.
smart_moves: Used objdiff-cli JSON as the data source instead of manually maintaining a translation-unit list.
Made the tool pipeline-friendly by supporting stdin via '-'.
Kept the initial tool narrowly scoped to Markdown generation while leaving room for later automation or parsing, as stated in the PR body.
Used Markdown links directly to source files so the wiki output remains actionable for contributors.
Included explicit assignee placeholders, turning progress-report data into a coordination surface for decomp contributors.
decomp_lessons: Progress and ownership data can be generated from existing objdiff reports, reducing manual wiki drift.
A simple script that converts match-report metadata into contributor-facing documentation can improve coordination without touching game code.
When generating wiki tables, preserve both quantitative progress metrics, such as matched_code, total_code, and fuzzy_match_percent, and coordination fields, such as assignees.
Small tooling PRs can be useful even if intentionally incomplete, as long as they establish a reusable data path for future automation.
naming_conventions: Tool file uses a concise snake_case name: tools/wiki_tu.py, where 'tu' abbreviates translation unit.
Report argument is named report_path in code and displayed as REPORT in argparse help.
Helper function friendly_size formats numeric fields from each translation-unit record.
The script expects objdiff unit keys including units, name, matched_code, total_code, fuzzy_match_percent, and complete.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly-level matching tactics were present.
The tooling consumes objdiff-cli match statistics, especially matched_code, total_code, fuzzy_match_percent, and complete, to summarize decompilation progress.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or formal reviews were included in the PR context.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that modify tools/wiki_tu.py or automate wiki translation-unit updates.
Search for uses of objdiff-cli JSON reports in other tooling.
Check whether the generated wiki table format was later expanded to parse assignees or update pages automatically.
Look for project documentation that references tools/wiki_tu.py.
